title: Trump Extends US–Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely; IRGC Seizes Vessels; US Issues 3–5 Day Ultimatum
detail: In a major diplomatic pivot announced on Truth Social on April 21 and taking effect April 22, President Trump extended the US–Iran ceasefire indefinitely — contingent on Iran submitting a 'unified proposal' for ending the nuclear standoff. The extension, made at Pakistan's request, forestalled an expected US military resumption but did not end coercive pressure. On April 22, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, which Al Jazeera's live blog confirmed, in apparent retaliation for the continuing US naval blockade. US officials simultaneously issued Iran a 3-to-5 day window to resolve internal factional divisions and begin substantive negotiations, warning strikes would resume if Iran failed to engage. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi condemned the ongoing blockade as 'an act of war.' An Iranian parliamentary adviser characterized the extension as 'a ploy to buy time for military escalation.' The core nuclear sticking point crystallized publicly: the US is demanding a 20-year pause on uranium enrichment, while Iran has offered only 5 years. The episode represents the continuation of what analysts have called structural coercive diplomacy: each party using the ceasefire to extract concessions rather than genuinely converge toward a framework agreement.

media.caption: Iran war day 54: Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely as IRGC seizes vessels in the Strait of Hormuz
Live updates: Trump extends Iran ceasefire as IRGC seizes ships and US issues ultimatum

title: Lebanon Formally Requests One-Month Ceasefire Extension at State Department Talks
detail: On April 22, Lebanon formally requested a one-month extension of the US-brokered 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire (which began April 16, 2026) at talks in Washington. Secretary of State Marco Rubio was expected to join the second round of direct Israel-Lebanon talks at the State Department. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam's primary demand remains full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory still under Israeli military presence. Both Israel and Lebanon have accused each other of ceasefire violations during the initial 10-day period. The ceasefire — negotiated under US auspices following the resumption of the Israeli-Lebanon front after Israel's strikes on Iran in late March 2026 — has held at the macro level despite reported skirmishes along the Blue Line. Analysts note that the Lebanon extension request is occurring simultaneously with the Iran nuclear diplomacy standoff, creating linkage pressure: a Lebanon deal would reduce one regional flashpoint as the Iran negotiations remain in flux. The Intercept reported that how Lebanon's ceasefire is handled could affect the prospects for a broader Iran deal.

media.caption: Lebanon formally requests one-month ceasefire extension at State Department talks with Secretary Rubio
